title: Document Bureau AI authority boundary
goal: Record that AI and local model output has no Bureau maintenance authority, expose the boundary in status projection, and keep Ollama out of Bureau core.

acceptance.assertion: docs/bureau-ai-authority-boundary-v1.md states that Ollama, Gemini and other LLM outputs do not establish queue truth, task truth, claim truth, merge readiness, runtime correctness, verification or completion.
status_projection emits an authority_boundary.ai section with deterministic_only core policy, advisory_only LLM output and forbidden effect classes.
AGENTS.md instructs agents not to add an Ollama Bureau secretary or treat AI output as authority without a later accepted benchmark and deterministic validator task.

verified_acceptance.ai-boundary-doc: docs/bureau-ai-authority-boundary-v1.md explicitly classifies Ollama, Gemini, copied LLM reviews and generated summaries as advisory-only and lists queue, registry, claim, verification, completion, merge, runtime and dispatch authority as forbidden effects.
verified_acceptance.status-projection-boundary: src/bureau/status_projection.py exposes authority_boundary.ai with deterministic_only core policy, advisory_only LLM output and forbidden effect classes; tests cover defensive copying and ai_authority non-claim output.
verified_acceptance.agent-contract-boundary: AGENTS.md forbids treating AI or local model output as queue truth, task verification, completion, claim truth, merge readiness, runtime correctness or dispatch authority and blocks an Ollama secretary without a later accepted benchmark and deterministic validator task.

validation: PR #280 CI validate run completed successfully before the registry closeout adjustment at head 61a8646307b9f607373e138275060b1f5c0c69b4.
Self-review found and fixed the duplicate-claim risk: T001 is verified in this registry change and is absent from active queue lanes; T002 is advanced as the next deterministic hardening task.
